alerts.title: Others Category Surged 1484% This Month
alerts.description: The 'Others' category jumped from ₹45,029 last month to ₹7,13,385 this month — a ₹6,68,355 increase. The top contributor is an unclassified UPI merchant (❗ UPI Txn) accounting for ₹4,69,910 across 47 transactions, making it impossible to assess necessity without better labelling.

alerts.title: Suspicious ₹99,999 'HDFC Account Update' Transaction
alerts.description: A single payment of ₹99,999 to 'View: Account Update For Your HDFC Bank' was recorded with only 1 occurrence. This phrasing is commonly associated with phishing or fraudulent UPI requests — verify this transaction with your bank immediately.

alerts.title: Food & Dining Up 75% — Zomato + Blinkit Leading
alerts.description: Food & Dining rose from ₹5,151 to ₹9,009 this month (+74.9%). Zomato alone accounts for ₹4,028 across 5 orders and Blinkit ₹4,981 across 3 orders, together making up ₹9,009 — nearly the entire category spend.

alerts.title: Shopping Jumped 169% to ₹13,831
alerts.description: Shopping spend rose from ₹5,131 last month to ₹13,831 this month. Amazon Pay (e-commerce) contributed ₹6,005 in a single transaction and Amazon Pay India ₹1,960, together accounting for over ₹7,965 of the spike.

tips: Label your UPI transactions: ₹4,69,910 across 47 transactions is categorised only as '❗ UPI Txn' — use a notes/tag feature in your banking app or a tracker like Walnut/Money View to classify these so you can actually analyse where 64% of your monthly spend is going.

Immediately verify the ₹99,999 payment to 'View: Account Update For Your HDFC Bank' — HDFC Bank never collects payments via UPI for account updates; this could be a fraud transaction and should be reported to HDFC helpline (1800-202-6161) and NPCI if unauthorised.

Your Zomato + Blinkit spend totalled ₹9,009 this month across just 8 orders — that's an average of ₹1,126 per order. Setting a monthly cap of ₹5,000 for these two apps combined and using Blinkit's scheduled delivery (to avoid impulse buys) could save ₹2,500–₹4,000/month.

The Medium Monthly subscription is being charged ₹5 (likely USD $0.05 or a token charge) — confirm whether this is an active, used subscription or a residual charge from a free trial that auto-converted, and cancel at medium.com/me/settings if unused to avoid it scaling to full price.

Transport spend fell from ₹4,660 to ₹1,841 this month (-60%) while Uber accounted for ₹1,805 across just 2 rides — averaging ₹903 per Uber trip. Consider Uber Pass or switching to Rapido/Namma Yatri for shorter trips to bring per-ride costs below ₹400.
